100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 15, 1926, the British government faced growing unrest as a nationwide general strike loomed, spurred by labor disputes and calls for better working conditions. In Germany, the political landscape grew increasingly volatile as the Weimar Republic struggled to maintain stability amidst economic challenges. Meanwhile, in the United States, the film industry continued to thrive with the rise of silent films, captivating audiences nationwide.
